def create_charts(df):
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
    
    # Sort the dataframe by Readmission Rate
    df_sorted = df.sort_values(by='Readmission Rate')
    
    # Create a bar chart for Length of Stay (Days) ordered by Readmission Rate
    fig1 = go.Figure()
    fig1.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=df_sorted['Readmission Rate'],
        y=df_sorted['Length of Stay (Days)'],
        marker=dict(color='#81FBA5'),
        name='Length of Stay (Days)'
    ))
    fig1.update_layout(
        title='Length of Stay vs. Readmission Rate',
        xaxis_title='Readmission Rate',
        yaxis_title='Length of Stay (Days)',
        template='plotly_white'
    )
    
    # Create a scatter plot with a trendline for Length of Stay vs. Readmission Rate
    fig2 = go.Figure()
    fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_sorted['Length of Stay (Days)'],
        y=df_sorted['Readmission Rate'],
        mode='markers',
        marker=dict(color='#44BFFC'),
        name='Data Points'
    ))
    # Add a trendline
    fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_sorted['Length of Stay (Days)'],
        y=df_sorted['Readmission Rate'],
        mode='lines',
        line=dict(color='#909BF5'),
        name='Trendline'
    ))
    fig2.update_layout(
        title='Scatter Plot of Length of Stay vs. Readmission Rate',
        xaxis_title='Length of Stay (Days)',
        yaxis_title='Readmission Rate',
        template='plotly_white'
    )
    
    return {
        "fig1": fig1,
        "fig2": fig2
    }
